'''Danny Lima''' (born 27 July 1975) is a Samoan former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 1990s and 2000s.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He was a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international.<ref name="England 38-14 Samoa">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/6103128.stm|title=England 38-14 Samoa|publisher=BBC|date=2006-11-05|access-date=2008-07-30}}</ref>

==Background==
Lima was born in [[Samoa]].

==Playing career==
Lima played at club level in Australasia for the [[Otahuhu Leopards]], the [[Auckland City Vulcans]], the [[Sydney Roosters]], the [[Canberra Raiders]], the [[Manly Sea Eagles]], the [[Northern Eagles]] with whom he played in the club's last ever game a 68–28 loss against Penrith and [[Melbourne Storm]], and in the [[Super League]] for the [[Warrington Wolves]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

Lima coached at [[Warrington Wizards]] before taking up a playing role in France.<ref name="Warrington Wizards History">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonwizards.org.uk/4.html|title=Warrington Wizards History|publisher=Warrington Wizards|date=2008-03-26|accessdate=2008-03-26|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080228183437/http://www.warringtonwizards.org.uk/4.html|archivedate=2008-02-28}}</ref> He played for [[RC Albi]] in France, but left after club went under liquidation, as well as [[SO Avignon]].<ref name="SO Avignon">{{cite web|url=http://www.sang-et-or.net/readarticle.php?article_id=412|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724054349/http://www.sang-et-or.net/readarticle.php?article_id=412|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 July 2011|title=SO Avignon|publisher=sang-et-or.net|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>
